items.name: Array
items.signature: public struct Array[T]: Iterable, ExpressibleByArrayLiteral, _ExpressibleByArrayLiteral, Cloneable, Defaultable
items.doc:
A dynamic growable array with copy-on-write semantics.

Arrays automatically grow when elements are added and use COW
to efficiently share data between copies until mutation occurs.

members.name: all
members.signature: public func all(satisfy predicate: (T) -> Bool) -> Bool
members.doc:
Returns true if all elements satisfy the predicate.

Returns true for an empty array (vacuous truth).
Short-circuits on first non-matching element.

Example:
    [2, 4, 6].all(satisfy: { (x) in x % 2 == 0 })  // true
    [2, 3, 6].all(satisfy: { (x) in x % 2 == 0 })  // false
    [].all(satisfy: { (x) in false })              // true (empty)

members.name: any
members.signature: public func any(satisfy predicate: (T) -> Bool) -> Bool
members.doc:
Returns true if any element satisfies the predicate.

Returns false for an empty array.
Short-circuits on first matching element.

Example:
    [1, 2, 3].any(satisfy: { (x) in x > 2 })  // true
    [1, 2, 3].any(satisfy: { (x) in x > 5 })  // false
    [].any(satisfy: { (x) in true })          // false (empty)

members.name: append
members.doc:
Adds an element to the end of the array.

Amortized O(1). May trigger reallocation if capacity is exceeded.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2]
    arr.append( 3)  // [1, 2, 3]

members.name: append
members.signature: public mutating func append(contentsOf other: Array[T])
members.doc:
Appends all elements from another array.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2]
    arr.append(contentsOf: [3, 4])  // [1, 2, 3, 4]

members.name: appendFrom
members.signature: public mutating func appendFrom[I](iterable: I) where I: Iterable, I.Item = T
members.doc:
Appends all elements from an iterable source.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2]
    arr.appendFrom(3..<6)  // [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

members.name: asPointer
members.doc:
Returns a raw pointer to the array's element storage.

WARNING: The pointer is invalidated by any mutation or reallocation.
Use with caution for FFI or low-level operations.

members.name: asSlice
chunks
members.signature: public func chunks(of size: Int64) -> ChunksIterator[T]

members.name: clear
members.doc:
Removes all elements from the array.

Capacity may be retained for reuse.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2, 3]
    arr.clear()  // arr is []

members.name: clone
members.doc: Creates a shallow clone (COW - copy deferred until mutation).

members.name: count
members.doc:
Returns the number of elements in the array.

Example:
    [1, 2, 3].count  // 3
    [].count         // 0

members.name: countWhere
members.doc:
Returns the number of elements satisfying the predicate.

Example:
    [1, 2, 3, 4, 5].countWhere({ (x) in x % 2 == 0 })  // 2

members.name: drop
members.signature: public func drop(first count: Int64) -> Slice[T]
members.doc:
Returns a slice with the first count elements removed.

Panics if count > self.count.

Example:
    [1, 2, 3, 4, 5].drop(first: 2)  // Slice[3, 4, 5]

members.name: drop
members.signature: public func drop(last count: Int64) -> Slice[T]
members.doc:
Returns a slice with the last count elements removed.

Panics if count > self.count.

Example:
    [1, 2, 3, 4, 5].drop(last: 2)  // Slice[1, 2, 3]

members.name: first
members.doc:
Returns the first element, or None if empty.

Example:
    [1, 2, 3].first()  // Some(1)
    [].first()         // None

members.name: first
members.signature: public func first(matching predicate: (T) -> Bool) -> T?
members.doc:
Returns the first element matching the predicate, or None.

Example:
    let arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    arr.first(matching: { (x) in x > 3 })  // Some(4)

members.name: firstIndex
members.signature: public func firstIndex(matching predicate: (T) -> Bool) -> Int64?
members.doc:
Returns the index of the first element matching the predicate, or None.

Example:
    let arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    arr.firstIndex(matching: { (x) in x > 3 })   // Some(3) - index of 4
    arr.firstIndex(matching: { (x) in x > 10 })  // None

members.name: indices
members.doc:
Returns the valid index range for this array.

Equivalent to `0..<count`. Useful for iteration or bounds checking.

Example:
    let arr = [10, 20, 30]
    arr.indices  // 0..<3

    for i in arr.indices {
        print(arr(i))
    }

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an empty array.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an empty array with the specified capacity.

members.name: init
members.doc: Internal initializer called by compiler for array literals.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an array from an array literal.

members.name: init
members.signature: public init(repeating value: T, count: Int64)
members.doc:
Creates an array by repeating a value count times.

Example:
    let zeros = Array(repeating: 0, count: 5)  // [0, 0, 0, 0, 0]
    let empty = Array(repeating: "x", count: 0)  // []

members.name: init
members.signature: public init[I](from iterable: I) where I: Iterable, I.Item = T
members.doc:
Creates an array from any iterable source.

Example:
    let fromRange = Array(from: 1..<5)  // [1, 2, 3, 4]
    let fromSet = Array(from: mySet)

members.name: init
members.doc:
Creates an array of count elements using a generator function.

The generator receives the index (0 to count-1) for each element.

Example:
    let squares = Array(count: 5, generator: { (i) in i * i })  // [0, 1, 4, 9, 16]
    let indices = Array(count: 3, generator: { (i) in i })  // [0, 1, 2]

members.name: insert
members.signature: public mutating func insert(element: T, at index: Int64)
members.doc:
Inserts an element at the specified index.

Shifts all elements from index onward to the right.
Panics if index > count (index == count appends to end).

Example:
    var arr = [1, 3]
    arr.insert(element: 2, at: 1)  // [1, 2, 3]
    arr.insert(element: 0, at: 0)  // [0, 1, 2, 3]
    arr.insert(element: 4, at: 4)  // [0, 1, 2, 3, 4] - append

members.name: isEmpty
members.doc:
Returns true if the array contains no elements.

Equivalent to `count == 0` but may be more readable.

Example:
    [].isEmpty      // true
    [1].isEmpty     // false

members.name: isValidIndex
members.doc:
Returns true if index is within valid bounds [0, count).

Example:
    let arr = [1, 2, 3]
    arr.isValidIndex(index: 0)   // true
    arr.isValidIndex(index: 2)   // true
    arr.isValidIndex(index: 3)   // false
    arr.isValidIndex(index: -1)  // false

members.name: iter
last
members.doc:
Returns the last element, or None if empty.

Example:
    [1, 2, 3].last()  // Some(3)
    [].last()         // None

members.name: last
members.signature: public func last(matching predicate: (T) -> Bool) -> T?
members.doc:
Returns the last element matching the predicate, or None.

Example:
    let arr = [1, 2, 3, 2, 1]
    arr.last(matching: { (x) in x > 1 })  // Some(2) - the second 2

members.name: lastIndex
members.signature: public func lastIndex(matching predicate: (T) -> Bool) -> Int64?
members.doc:
Returns the index of the last element matching the predicate, or None.

Example:
    let arr = [1, 2, 3, 2, 1]
    arr.lastIndex(matching: { (x) in x == 2 })  // Some(3)

members.name: partition
members.signature: public mutating func partition(by predicate: (T) -> Bool) -> Int64

members.name: partitioned
members.signature: public func partitioned(by predicate: (T) -> Bool) -> (Array[T], Array[T])

members.name: pop
members.doc:
Removes and returns the last element, or None if empty.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2, 3]
    arr.pop()  // Some(3), arr is [1, 2]
    arr.pop()  // Some(2), arr is [1]
    arr.pop()  // Some(1), arr is []
    arr.pop()  // None, arr is still []

members.name: popFirst
members.doc:
Removes and returns the first element, or None if empty.

Note: O(n) as all elements must shift left.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2, 3]
    arr.popFirst()  // Some(1), arr is [2, 3]

members.name: prefix
members.doc:
Returns a slice of the first count elements.

Panics if count > self.count.

Example:
    [1, 2, 3, 4, 5].prefix(count: 3)  // Slice[1, 2, 3]
    [1, 2].prefix(count: 0)           // Empty slice

members.name: remove
members.signature: public mutating func remove(at index: Int64) -> T
members.doc:
Removes and returns the element at the specified index.

Shifts subsequent elements left. Panics if index is out of bounds.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2, 3, 4]
    arr.remove(at: 1)  // returns 2, arr is [1, 3, 4]

members.name: removeAll
members.signature: public mutating func removeAll(matching predicate: (T) -> Bool)
members.doc:
Removes all elements that satisfy the predicate.

The inverse of `retain`. Elements are visited in order.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    arr.removeAll(matching: { (x) in x % 2 == 0 })  // arr is [1, 3, 5]

    var names = ["Alice", "", "Bob", ""]
    names.removeAll(matching: |s| s.isEmpty)  // ["Alice", "Bob"]

members.name: removeSubrange
members.doc:
Removes elements in the specified range.

Panics if range is out of bounds.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    arr.removeSubrange(range: 1..<4)  // arr is [1, 5]

members.name: replaceSubrange
members.signature: public mutating func replaceSubrange(range: Range[Int64], with replacement: Array[T])
members.doc:
Replaces elements in the specified range with elements from replacement.

The replacement can have a different length than the range.
Panics if range is out of bounds.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    arr.replaceSubrange(range: 1..<4, with: [20, 30])  // [1, 20, 30, 5]

members.name: reserveCapacity
retain
members.signature: public mutating func retain(matching predicate: (T) -> Bool)
members.doc:
Retains only elements that satisfy the predicate.

Elements are visited in order. This is an in-place filter.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    arr.retain(matching: { (x) in x % 2 == 0 })  // arr is [2, 4]

members.name: reverse
members.doc:
Reverses the order of elements in place.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2, 3]
    arr.reverse()  // [3, 2, 1]

members.name: reversed
members.doc:
Returns a new array with elements in reversed order.

The original array is unchanged.

Example:
    let arr = [1, 2, 3]
    let rev = arr.reversed()  // [3, 2, 1]
    // arr is still [1, 2, 3]

members.name: rotate
members.signature: public mutating func rotate(by amount: Int64)
members.doc:
Rotates elements left by the given amount.

Positive amounts rotate left (first elements move to end).
Negative amounts rotate right (last elements move to start).

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    arr.rotate(by: 2)   // [3, 4, 5, 1, 2]
    arr.rotate(by: -1)  // [2, 3, 4, 5, 1]

members.name: setUnchecked
members.signature: public mutating func setUnchecked(index: Int64, value: T)
members.doc: Sets the element at the given index without bounds checking.

members.name: shrinkToFit
members.doc:
Reduces capacity to match the current count.

Frees excess memory. Useful after removing many elements.

Example:
    var arr = Array[Int64](capacity: 1000)
    arr.append( 1)
    arr.shrinkToFit()  // capacity reduced to ~1

members.name: shuffle
members.signature: public mutating func shuffle[R](using rng: R) where R: RandomNumberGenerator
members.doc:
Randomly shuffles the array in place using the provided generator.

Uses the Fisher-Yates shuffle algorithm.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    var rng = Lcg64(seed: 42)
    arr.shuffle(using: rng)  // deterministic shuffle

members.name: shuffle
members.doc:
Randomly shuffles the array in place.

Uses the default random number generator.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    arr.shuffle()  // e.g., [3, 1, 5, 2, 4]

members.name: shuffled
members.signature: public func shuffled[R](using rng: R) -> Array[T] where R: RandomNumberGenerator

members.name: shuffled
members.doc:
Returns a new array with elements in random order.

Uses the default random number generator.
The original array is unchanged.

Example:
    let arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    let shuffled = arr.shuffled()  // e.g., [4, 2, 5, 1, 3]
    // arr is still [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

members.name: subscript
members.doc:
Accesses the element at the given index.

Panics if index is out of bounds [0, count).

Example:
    let arr = [10, 20, 30]
    arr(0)      // 10
    arr(1) = 25 // arr is now [10, 25, 30]
    arr(5)      // PANIC: index out of bounds

members.name: suffix
members.doc:
Returns a slice of the last count elements.

Panics if count > self.count.

Example:
    [1, 2, 3, 4, 5].suffix(count: 2)  // Slice[4, 5]

members.name: swap
members.signature: public mutating func swap(at i: Int64, with j: Int64)
members.doc:
Swaps the elements at the two given indices.

Panics if either index is out of bounds.

Example:
    var arr = [1, 2, 3]
    arr.swap(at: 0, with: 2)  // [3, 2, 1]

members.doc: Creates an array iterator.

members.name: next
members.doc: Returns the next element, or None if exhausted.

items.name: DefaultHasher
items.doc:
Default hasher using the FNV-1a algorithm.

FNV-1a is a non-cryptographic hash function created by Glenn Fowler,
Landon Curt Noll, and Phong Vo. It's fast and has good distribution
properties for hash table use.

members.name: finish
members.doc: Finishes hashing and returns the final hash value.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates a new hasher with the FNV-1a offset basis.

members.name: write
members.doc: Writes bytes into the hasher state.

items.name: Dictionary
items.signature: public struct Dictionary[K, V, H = DefaultHasher]: Iterable, Cloneable where K: Hash, H: Hasher, H: Defaultable
items.doc:
A hash map with copy-on-write semantics.

Uses open addressing with linear probing. Keys must implement Hash.

members.name: all
members.signature: public func all(satisfy predicate: (K, V) -> Bool) -> Bool
members.doc:
Returns true if all entries satisfy the predicate.

Returns true for an empty dictionary (vacuous truth).

Example:
    ["a": 2, "b": 4].all(satisfy: { (k, v) in v % 2 == 0 })  // true
    ["a": 1, "b": 2].all(satisfy: { (k, v) in v % 2 == 0 })  // false
    [:].all(satisfy: { (k, v) in false })                    // true

members.name: any
members.signature: public func any(satisfy predicate: (K, V) -> Bool) -> Bool
members.doc:
Returns true if any entry satisfies the predicate.

Alias for `contains(where:)`.

Example:
    ["a": 1, "b": 5].any(satisfy: { (k, v) in v > 3 })  // true

members.name: clear
members.doc:
Removes all entries from the dictionary.

Capacity may be retained for reuse.

Example:
    var dict = ["a": 1, "b": 2]
    dict.clear()  // dict is now [:]

members.name: clone
members.signature: public func clone() -> Dictionary[K, V, H]
members.doc: Creates a shallow clone (COW - copy deferred until mutation).

members.name: compactMapValues
members.signature: public func compactMapValues[U](transform: (V) -> U?) -> Dictionary[K, U, H]

members.name: contains
members.doc: Returns true if the dictionary contains the given key.

members.name: contains
members.signature: public func contains(matching predicate: (K, V) -> Bool) -> Bool
members.doc:
Returns true if any entry satisfies the predicate.

Returns false for an empty dictionary.
Short-circuits on first matching entry.

Example:
    ["a": 1, "b": 5].contains(where: { (k, v) in v > 3 })  // true
    ["a": 1, "b": 2].contains(where: { (k, v) in v > 3 })  // false

members.name: count
members.doc:
Returns the number of key-value pairs in the dictionary.

Example:
    ["a": 1, "b": 2].count  // 2
    [:].count               // 0

members.name: countWhere
members.signature: public func countWhere(predicate: (K, V) -> Bool) -> Int64
members.doc:
Returns the count of entries satisfying the predicate.

Example:
    ["a": 1, "b": 2, "c": 3].countWhere({ (k, v) in v > 1 })  // 2

members.name: filter
members.signature: public func filter(matching predicate: (K, V) -> Bool) -> Dictionary[K, V, H]
members.doc:
Returns a new dictionary with only entries satisfying the predicate.

Example:
    let dict = ["a": 1, "b": 2, "c": 3]
    let big = dict.filter(where: { (k, v) in v > 1 })  // ["b": 2, "c": 3]

members.name: first
members.signature: public func first(matching predicate: (K, V) -> Bool) -> (K, V)?
members.doc:
Returns the first entry matching the predicate, or None.

Note: Since dictionary order is unspecified, "first" is arbitrary.

Example:
    let dict = ["a": 1, "b": 5, "c": 3]
    dict.first(where: { (k, v) in v > 2 })  // Some entry with v > 2

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an empty dictionary.

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates an empty dictionary with initial capacity.

members.name: init
members.signature: public init[I](from pairs: I) where I: Iterable, I.Item = (K, V)
members.doc:
Creates a dictionary from an iterable of key-value tuples.

If duplicate keys exist, later values overwrite earlier ones.

Example:
    let pairs = [("a", 1), ("b", 2)]
    let dict = Dictionary(from: pairs)  // ["a": 1, "b": 2]

members.name: init
members.signature:
public init[I, E](grouping elements: I, by keyFunc: (E) -> K)
        where I: Iterable, I.Item = E, V = Array[E]
members.doc:
Creates a dictionary by grouping elements by a key function.

Each key maps to an array of all elements that produced that key.
Note: This initializer requires V = Array[E].

Example:
    let words = ["apple", "apricot", "banana", "blueberry"]
    let grouped = Dictionary(grouping: words, by: { (w) in w.chars.first().unwrap() })
    // ["a": ["apple", "apricot"], "b": ["banana", "blueberry"]]

members.name: init
members.signature: public init[I](uniqueKeysWithValues pairs: I) where I: Iterable, I.Item = (K, V)
members.doc:
Creates a dictionary from unique key-value pairs.

Panics if duplicate keys are encountered. Use `init(from:)` if
duplicates should be allowed (with later values winning).

Example:
    let dict = Dictionary(uniqueKeysWithValues: [("a", 1), ("b", 2)])

members.name: insert
members.signature: public mutating func insert(key: K, value: V) -> V?
members.doc:
Inserts or updates a value for the given key.

Returns the old value if the key already existed.

members.name: iter
members.doc: Returns an iterator over key-value pairs.

members.name: keys
members.signature: public var keys: KeysView[K, V] { get

members.name: mapValues
members.signature: public func mapValues[U](transform: (V) -> U) -> Dictionary[K, U, H]
members.doc:
Returns a new dictionary with transformed values.

Keys are preserved; only values are transformed.

Example:
    let dict = ["a": 1, "b": 2]
    let doubled = dict.mapValues { it * 2 }  // ["a": 2, "b": 4]

members.name: merge
members.signature: public mutating func merge(other: Dictionary[K, V, H], uniquingKeysWith combine: (V, V) -> V)
members.doc:
Merges another dictionary into this one.

For duplicate keys, the combine function determines the resulting value.

Example:
    var a = ["x": 1, "y": 2]
    let b = ["y": 20, "z": 30]
    a.merge( b, uniquingKeysWith: { (old, new) in old + new })
    // a is now ["x": 1, "y": 22, "z": 30]

members.name: mergeFrom
members.signature:
public mutating func mergeFrom[I](pairs: I, uniquingKeysWith combine: (V, V) -> V)
        where I: Iterable, I.Item = (K, V)
members.doc:
Merges key-value pairs from an iterable into this dictionary.

Example:
    var dict = ["a": 1]
    dict.mergeFrom([("b", 2), ("c", 3)], uniquingKeysWith: { (_, new) in new })

members.name: merging
members.signature:
public func merging(other: Dictionary[K, V, H], uniquingKeysWith combine: (V, V) -> V)
        -> Dictionary[K, V, H]
members.doc:
Returns a new dictionary merging this one with another.

This dictionary is unchanged.

Example:
    let a = ["x": 1, "y": 2]
    let b = ["y": 20, "z": 30]
    let merged = a.merging(other: b, uniquingKeysWith: { (_, new) in new })
    // merged is ["x": 1, "y": 20, "z": 30]

members.name: remove
members.signature: public mutating func remove(key: K) -> V?
members.doc: Removes the key and returns its value, or None if not found.

members.name: removeAll
members.signature: public mutating func removeAll(matching predicate: (K, V) -> Bool)
members.doc:
Removes all entries that satisfy the predicate.

The inverse of `retain(where:)`.

Example:
    var dict = ["a": 1, "b": 2, "c": 3]
    dict.removeAll(where: { (k, v) in v < 2 })  // ["b": 2, "c": 3]

members.name: reserveCapacity
members.doc:
Reserves capacity for at least minimumCapacity entries.

Does nothing if current capacity is already sufficient.

Example:
    var dict = Dictionary[String, Int64]()
    dict.reserveCapacity(minimumCapacity: 1000)

members.name: retain
members.signature: public mutating func retain(matching predicate: (K, V) -> Bool)
members.doc:
Retains only entries that satisfy the predicate.

Example:
    var dict = ["a": 1, "b": 2, "c": 3]
    dict.retain(where: { (k, v) in v > 1 })  // ["b": 2, "c": 3]

members.name: shrinkToFit
members.doc:
Reduces capacity to match the current count.

Frees excess memory. Useful after removing many entries.

Example:
    var dict = Dictionary[String, Int64](capacity: 1000)
    dict("a") = 1
    dict.shrinkToFit()  // capacity reduced

members.name: storage
members.signature: private var storage: RcBox[DictionaryStorage[K, V, H

members.signature:
public subscript(key: K, default defaultValue: V) -> V {
        get

members.name: subscript
members.signature:
public subscript(unwrap key: K) -> V {
        get

members.name: update
members.signature: public mutating func update(key: K, with transform: (V) -> V) -> Bool
members.doc:
Updates the value for an existing key using a transform function.

Returns true if the key existed and was updated, false otherwise.

Example:
    var dict = ["a": 1, "b": 2]
    dict.update(key: "a", with: { (v) in v * 10 })  // true, dict["a"] = 10
    dict.update(key: "z", with: { (v) in v * 10 })  // false, no change

members.name: upsert
members.signature: public mutating func upsert(key: K, default defaultValue: V, with transform: (V) -> V)
members.doc:
Updates or inserts: applies transform if key exists, otherwise inserts default.

Example:
    var counts: [String: Int64] = [:]
    counts.upsert(key: "apple", default: 0, with: { (n) in n + 1 })
    counts.upsert(key: "apple", default: 0, with: { (n) in n + 1 })
    counts("apple")  // Some(2)

members.signature: public var values: ValuesView[K, V] { get

items.name: Set
items.signature: public struct Set[T, H = DefaultHasher]: Iterable, Cloneable where T: Hash, H: Hasher, H: Defaultable
items.doc:
A hash set that stores unique elements with copy-on-write semantics.

Sets provide O(1) average-case lookup, insertion, and removal. They use
COW for efficient copying - the underlying storage is only duplicated
when a shared set is mutated.

Backed by a Dictionary internally.

Example:
    var fruits: Set = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
    fruits.insert( "date")
    fruits.contains( "apple")  // true
    fruits.remove( "banana")

Set literals use array syntax with type annotation:
    let empty: Set[Int64] = []
    let numbers: Set = [1, 2, 3]

members.name: all
members.signature: public func all(satisfy predicate: (T) -> Bool) -> Bool
members.doc:
Returns true if all elements satisfy the predicate.

Returns true for an empty set (vacuous truth).

Example:
    Set([2, 4, 6]).all(satisfy: { (x) in x % 2 == 0 })  // true
    Set([1, 2, 4]).all(satisfy: { (x) in x % 2 == 0 })  // false
    Set[Int64]().all(satisfy: { (x) in false })         // true

members.name: any
members.signature: public func any(satisfy predicate: (T) -> Bool) -> Bool
members.doc:
Returns true if any element satisfies the predicate.

Alias for `contains(where:)`.

Example:
    Set([1, 2, 3]).any(satisfy: { (x) in x > 2 })  // true

members.name: clear
members.doc:
Removes all elements from the set.

Capacity may be retained for reuse.

Example:
    var set: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    set.clear()  // set is {}

members.name: clone
members.doc:
Creates a shallow clone of the set.

Due to COW semantics, this is O(1) - the actual copy is deferred
until either set is mutated.

members.name: compactMap
members.signature: public func compactMap[U](transform: (T) -> U?) -> Set[U, H] where U: Hash
members.doc:
Returns a new set with elements transformed, removing None results.

Example:
    let set: Set = ["1", "two", "3"]
    let nums = set.compactMap(transform: { (s) in Int64.parse(s) })  // {1, 3}

members.name: contains
members.signature: public func contains(matching predicate: (T) -> Bool) -> Bool
members.doc:
Returns true if any element satisfies the predicate.

Returns false for an empty set.
Short-circuits on first matching element.

Example:
    Set([1, 2, 3]).contains(where: { (x) in x > 2 })  // true
    Set([1, 2, 3]).contains(where: { (x) in x > 5 })  // false

members.name: count
members.doc:
Returns the number of elements in the set.

Example:
    Set([1, 2, 3]).count  // 3
    Set[Int64]().count    // 0

members.name: countWhere
members.doc:
Returns the count of elements satisfying the predicate.

Example:
    Set([1, 2, 3, 4, 5]).countWhere({ (x) in x % 2 == 0 })  // 2

members.name: difference
members.signature: public func difference(other: Set[T, H]) -> Set[T, H]
members.doc:
Returns a new set containing elements in this set but not in the other.

Also known as "subtracting" the other set.

Example:
    let a: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    let b: Set = [2, 3, 4]
    a.difference(other: b)  // {1}

members.name: filter
members.signature: public func filter(matching predicate: (T) -> Bool) -> Set[T, H]
members.doc:
Returns a new set with only elements satisfying the predicate.

Example:
    let set: Set = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    let evens = set.filter(where: { (x) in x % 2 == 0 })  // {2, 4}

members.name: first
members.signature: public func first(matching predicate: (T) -> Bool) -> T?
members.doc:
Returns the first element matching the predicate, or None.

Note: Since set order is unspecified, "first" is arbitrary.

Example:
    let set: Set = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    set.first(where: { (x) in x > 3 })  // Some(4) or Some(5)

members.name: flatMap
members.signature: public func flatMap[U](transform: (T) -> Set[U, H]) -> Set[U, H] where U: Hash

members.name: formDifference
members.signature: public mutating func formDifference(other: Set[T, H])
members.doc:
Removes elements that are in the other set (mutating difference).

Example:
    var a: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    let b: Set = [2, 3, 4]
    a.formDifference(other: b)  // a is {1}

members.name: formIntersection
members.signature: public mutating func formIntersection(other: Set[T, H])
members.doc:
Removes elements not in the other set (mutating intersection).

Example:
    var a: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    let b: Set = [2, 3, 4]
    a.formIntersection(other: b)  // a is {2, 3}

members.name: formSymmetricDifference
members.signature: public mutating func formSymmetricDifference(other: Set[T, H])
members.doc:
Replaces this set with the symmetric difference (mutating).

Example:
    var a: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    let b: Set = [2, 3, 4]
    a.formSymmetricDifference(other: b)  // a is {1, 4}

members.name: formUnion
members.signature: public mutating func formUnion(other: Set[T, H])
members.doc:
Adds the elements of another set to this set (mutating union).

Example:
    var a: Set = [1, 2]
    let b: Set = [2, 3]
    a.formUnion( b)  // a is {1, 2, 3}

members.name: getDict
members.doc: Returns the internal dictionary (for extensions).

members.name: init
members.doc:
Creates an empty set with the specified initial capacity.

Pre-allocating capacity can improve performance when the approximate
final size is known, avoiding repeated reallocations.

Example:
    var set = Set[String](capacity: 1000)
    set.capacity  // >= 1000
    set.count     // 0

members.name: init
members.signature: public init[I](from elements: I) where I: Iterable, I.Item = T
members.doc:
Creates a set from an iterable of elements.

Duplicate elements are automatically removed.

Example:
    let arr = [1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3]
    let set = Set(from: arr)  // {1, 2, 3}

members.name: init
members.doc: Creates a set from an array literal (used by compiler).

members.name: insert
members.signature: public mutating func insert(element: T) -> Bool
members.doc:
Inserts an element into the set.

Returns true if the element was newly inserted, false if it already existed.

Example:
    var set: Set = [1, 2]
    set.insert( 3)  // true, set is {1, 2, 3}
    set.insert( 2)  // false, already present

members.name: insert
members.signature: public mutating func insert[I](contentsOf elements: I) where I: Iterable, I.Item = T
members.doc:
Inserts all elements from an iterable into the set.

Example:
    var set: Set = [1, 2]
    set.insert(contentsOf: [3, 4, 5])  // {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}

members.name: intersection
members.signature: public func intersection(other: Set[T, H]) -> Set[T, H]
members.doc:
Returns a new set containing only elements present in both sets.

Example:
    let a: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    let b: Set = [2, 3, 4]
    a.intersection(other: b)  // {2, 3}

members.name: isDisjoint
members.signature: public func isDisjoint(with other: Set[T, H]) -> Bool
members.doc:
Returns true if this set and the other share no common elements.

Example:
    let a: Set = [1, 2]
    let b: Set = [3, 4]
    let c: Set = [2, 3]
    a.isDisjoint(with: b)  // true
    a.isDisjoint(with: c)  // false

members.name: isStrictSubset
members.signature: public func isStrictSubset(of other: Set[T, H]) -> Bool
members.doc:
Returns true if this set is a subset of the other but not equal.

Example:
    let a: Set = [1, 2]
    let b: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    a.isStrictSubset(of: b)  // true
    a.isStrictSubset(of: a)  // false

members.name: isStrictSuperset
members.signature: public func isStrictSuperset(of other: Set[T, H]) -> Bool
members.doc:
Returns true if this set is a superset of the other but not equal.

Example:
    let a: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    let b: Set = [1, 2]
    a.isStrictSuperset(of: b)  // true
    a.isStrictSuperset(of: a)  // false

members.name: isSubset
members.signature: public func isSubset(of other: Set[T, H]) -> Bool
members.doc:
Returns true if all elements of this set are in the other set.

A set is always a subset of itself.

Example:
    let a: Set = [1, 2]
    let b: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    a.isSubset(of: b)  // true
    b.isSubset(of: a)  // false
    a.isSubset(of: a)  // true

members.name: isSuperset
members.signature: public func isSuperset(of other: Set[T, H]) -> Bool
members.doc:
Returns true if all elements of the other set are in this set.

A set is always a superset of itself.

Example:
    let a: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    let b: Set = [1, 2]
    a.isSuperset(of: b)  // true
    b.isSuperset(of: a)  // false

members.name: iter
map
members.signature: public func map[U](transform: (T) -> U) -> Set[U, H] where U: Hash

members.name: remove
members.signature: public mutating func remove(element: T) -> Bool
members.doc:
Removes an element from the set.

Returns true if the element was present and removed, false otherwise.

Example:
    var set: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    set.remove( 2)  // true, set is {1, 3}
    set.remove( 5)  // false, not present

members.name: removeAll
members.signature: public mutating func removeAll(matching predicate: (T) -> Bool)
members.doc:
Removes all elements that satisfy the predicate.

The inverse of `retain(where:)`.

Example:
    var set: Set = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    set.removeAll(where: { (x) in x % 2 == 0 })  // {1, 3, 5}

members.name: reserveCapacity
members.doc:
Reserves capacity for at least minimumCapacity elements.

Does nothing if current capacity is already sufficient.

Example:
    var set = Set[String]()
    set.reserveCapacity( 1000)

members.name: retain
members.signature: public mutating func retain(matching predicate: (T) -> Bool)
members.doc:
Retains only elements that satisfy the predicate.

Example:
    var set: Set = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
    set.retain(where: { (x) in x % 2 == 0 })  // {2, 4}

members.name: shrinkToFit
members.doc:
Reduces capacity to match the current count.

Frees excess memory. Useful after removing many elements.

Example:
    var set = Set[String](capacity: 1000)
    set.insert( "a")
    set.shrinkToFit()  // capacity reduced

members.name: symmetricDifference
members.signature: public func symmetricDifference(other: Set[T, H]) -> Set[T, H]
members.doc:
Returns a new set containing elements in either set but not both.

Example:
    let a: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    let b: Set = [2, 3, 4]
    a.symmetricDifference(other: b)  // {1, 4}

members.name: toArray
members.doc:
Returns an array containing all elements of the set.

The order of elements in the array is unspecified.

Example:
    let set: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    let arr = set.toArray()  // [1, 2, 3] in some order

members.name: union
members.signature: public func union(other: Set[T, H]) -> Set[T, H]
members.doc:
Returns a new set containing all elements from both sets.

Example:
    let a: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    let b: Set = [3, 4, 5]
    a.union(other: b)  // {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}

items.name: SetIterator
items.signature: public struct SetIterator[T, H = DefaultHasher]: Iterator where T: Hash, H: Hasher, H: Defaultable
items.doc:
Iterator for Set that yields elements sequentially.

Obtained by calling `iter()` on a set. Typically used implicitly
via for-in loops or iterator methods.

Example:
    let set: Set = [1, 2, 3]
    for item in set {
        print(item)
    }

members.name: next
members.doc: Returns the next element, or None if exhausted.
